_fixture_note: Real output from 2026-07-16 against a deliberately-built test job (dep_optimizer_test_job, job_id 619066184056874): root task then 3 independent sibling tasks (branch_a, branch_b, branch_c, each sleeping ~25s), all sharing one deliberately small single-node cluster to try to force resource contention. Real result: Databricks ran all 3 independent tasks fully concurrently anyway (start times within ~220ms of each other) -- the attempt to force serialization did not succeed, an honest result in itself. Critical path (root+branch_a, 380.2s estimated) matched the real observed average job duration (381.1s) within 0.9s, confirming the calculation is accurate against real timestamps.
detail: All 3 independent pair(s) with enough run evidence were confirmed running concurrently in at least one real run -- no serialization issue found.
